disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent falls — three episodes of coming to rest on the ground over the past month, with a recently unsteady, slow, wide-based walking pattern
trigger_question_examples: How has her walking and balance been lately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: This is the third time she's ended up on the floor this past month. She's been really unsteady and slow when she walks lately.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: After this fall she is on the floor and cannot bear weight or get herself up without help
trigger_question_examples: Can she stand or get up at all?
Is she able to put weight on her legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — she's stuck on the floor and can't push herself up at all, and she can't get any strength into her legs to stand.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Proximal lower-limb weakness — legs 'give way', marked difficulty rising from a chair or the floor
trigger_question_examples: What happens with her legs when this occurs?
Does she struggle to stand up from chairs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Her legs just give way, especially her thighs, and she's been finding it really hard to get up out of chairs.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Polypharmacy — a recently started night-time sleeping tablet plus two blood-pressure tablets and other regular medicines
trigger_question_examples: Have any of her tablets changed recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She takes a sleeping tablet the doctor started a few weeks ago, two blood-pressure tablets, and a few other regular tablets.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives around the fall — denies dizziness, lightheadedness or greying of vision on standing before the fall (it happened on turning)
trigger_question_examples: Did she feel dizzy or faint before she fell?
Did it come on when she stood up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, she didn't feel dizzy or faint — it happened when she turned quickly, her legs just gave way.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives for immediate danger — did not hit her head, did not lose consciousness, no severe hip or groin pain reported
trigger_question_examples: Did she hit her head or black out?
Is she in severe pain anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She doesn't think she hit her head and she didn't black out — she remembers it all. She's sore but not screaming in pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Social and safety context — lives alone, has a strong fear of falling with self-imposed activity restriction, loose rugs and poor lighting in the home
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone able to stay with her?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She lives on her own. The neighbour found her. She's been so scared of falling that she's hardly moved around the house this week.
